title: Borderlands
notes: One thing you need to do: run the game and exit. And you're done. Optional: You can tweak graphics/mouse smoothig for performance via game configuration file, just like in Windows, because game is not well running even in native Windows. You can find these files in "/home/<YourUsername>/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/8980/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/my games/borderlands/WillowGame/Config". But even without this, Borderlands is very stable and works excelent.
specs: Intel® Core™2 Quad CPU Q9550 / GTX 760 2GB

title: Borderlands
notes: There is a performance hit with shadows. This same issue was present in the game running on Windows 10 on several machines, both mine and others. There are additionally mouse sensitivity issues and some judder in animations when they are made for the first time, or when new areas are visited.

title: Borderlands
notes: In the binaries folder, "cudart.dll" needs to be copied to "physxcudart_20.dll" or the game will complain about a missing Borderlands.exe file. Afterwards, the game runs completely fine, no issues or flaws at all!

title: Borderlands
notes:
The game crahed on first launch with the error message:
General protection fault! History: Address = 0xaae5fa (filename not found)
To fix this you have to create a copy (!) of the cudart.dll and rename it to physxcudart_20.dll. Both cudart.dll and physxcudart_20.dll have to be in the Borderlands/Binaries directory. Without this the game does not start. After that there are no issues.
Game played with no issues in default configuration.

notes: Played through entire game.  Performance is great.  Crashes occasionally but infrequent enough that it is still playable

title: Borderlands

notes: Mouse spinning hits an invisible wall - resolved if I drop to 1680x1050 resolution.
title: Borderlands

notes: Played from level 1 to level 69 (PT 2.5 including all DLCs). Performance was good and had no issues regarding mouse or sound. However, I did experience random crashes throughout the game. Those were especially punishing in the DLCs which only have a single fast travel station. Which means sometimes you travel across two maps, clear an entire area and then it crashes, meaning you have to do everything again. From my experience the crashes were totally random (sometimes it would run fine for two hours straight, sometimes it would crash three times in 10 minutes). All in all I still consider it playable, but the crashes certainly dampened the mood for me.
title: Borderlands

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX 560 Series (POLARIS11, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.12-zen1-1-zen, LLVM 7.0.0)
notes: Initially low framerates, but after setting 'AllowD3D10=True' in WillowGame.ini I saw serious improvement. Still a performance hit depending on the area and where I look, but it's entirely playable. Adjusting graphics options did not help.

notes: Would only work with the option set to use specific proton, set to 3.7-8 (not tried any of the newer ones), Without that option it would launch but you could not see it. Minor performance issues, little bit laggy, but playable. (changing GFX settings makes no difference)
title: Borderlands

notes: There's an occasional mouse bug that you just need to adjust sensitivity in the settings to make it normal again. Otherwise, hours upon hours of nostalgic enjoyment.
title: Borderlands

notes: starts fine, plays fine on existing character, but crashed on making a new character during the first loading screen(when you get of the bus)
title: Borderlands

notes: Game is just shy of a platinum rating. The only issue I've found is that sometimes, if you alt-tab away from the game and then back, the mouse will hit the edge of the display instead of moving forever. Others have noted the same problem under different descriptions (eg "Mouse spinning hits an invisible wall - resolved if I drop to 1680x1050 resolution"). Work-around is simple - just alt-tab again and come back in. Game is extremely playable, significantly better than trying to run Steam itself in vanilla Wine (which was already mostly-playable and is how I originally played the game).
title: Borderlands

notes: Don't have really good frame rate but work fine
title: Borderlands

notes: Works everything out of the box, no tweak required. Tested in about 5-6 hours of Internet multiplayer with friends.
title: Borderlands

notes: Switching windows with Alt+Tab might cause issues: when return to the game, you might no longer be able to fully turn around (360 degrees). One way to fix this is to switch windows again as many times as necessary. Apart from that, works perfectly !
title: Borderlands

notes: Runs great using 3.7-8 and 3.16-4. Dynamic Shadows drops framerate down into the mid 40s at times.
title: Borderlands

notes: Funciona tudo perfeitamente. Inclusive o controle do xbox 360.
title: Borderlands

notes: Only issue I have is tabbing out, returning back to the game as others have said somehow stops your ability to 360 rotate in game, but changing any of the video settings immediately fixes this. Played a good 6 hours, online, and LAN. All GFX up to the highest and runs smooth and fine, mouse acceleration is a bit bad, but bearable, occasional white flashes, but they are barely noticeable.

notes: cp cudart.dll physxcudart_20.dll

notes: cp cudart.dll physxcudart_20.dll
title: Borderlands

notes: Overall, this is an improvement over 3.16-5.  The game play is more fluid with less frequent frame rate drops.  However, I am rating it as silver due to anisotropic filtering must be disabled, otherwise the game will randomly crash.
title: Borderlands

notes: played multiplayer up to level 30; game is kind of sensitive to tabbing out (2-3 crashes when in background); keeps capturing the mouse cursor; steam overlay has invisible cursor
